source: ExchangeRate-API (open.er-api.com) mid-market rates 17:08 UTC, cross-referenced with Wise and Trading Economics
baseNote: Rates show ZMW per 1 unit of foreign currency. ↑ ZMW = Kwacha weakening (depreciating).
mpRateNote: Monetary Policy Rate held at 13.50% — reduced 75bps from 14.25% on Feb 11, 2026. BOZ held rate at May 2026 review. Next review: August 2026.

USD.note: OpenExchangeRates Jun 18: 1 USD = 17.784 ZMW. Wise mid-market shows 17.95, Trading Economics 17.76. Kwacha slightly softer from 17.74 (Jun 16).

GBP.note: 1 GBP = 23.894 ZMW (OpenExRates Jun 18). Sterling firming against kwacha.

EUR.note: 1 EUR = 20.663 ZMW (OpenExRates Jun 18). Euro slightly higher vs kwacha from Jun 16's 20.60.

ZAR.note: 1 ZAR = 1.101 ZMW (OpenExRates Jun 18). Rand nearly flat from Jun 16's 1.096. 1 ZMW = 0.908 ZAR.

BWP.note: Botswana pula. Estimated cross rate via OpenExRates: 1 BWP = 1.307 ZMW.

trendAnalysis.direction: Kwacha slightly softer — USD/ZMW 17.78 (OpenExRates), up from 17.74 (Jun 16)
drivers: Record copper output 890K tonnes (2025) — strong export earnings
Record maize harvest >4M tonnes — reduced food import needs
Debt restructuring 94% complete — sovereign risk premium collapsing
IMF successor programme talks scheduled July 2026 — staff visit confirmed
MPR at 13.5% — high real yields attracting carry trade inflows
Yuan mining tax corridor reducing USD demand
Gross international reserves at historic high of US$6.5 billion
trendAnalysis.outlook: Kwacha consolidating after pullback from Jun 11 peak (17.46). Mild day-over-day softening suggests profit-taking continues. Copper prices and IMF programme progress remain key catalysts. BOZ leaning against excessive strength to protect export competitiveness.
trendAnalysis.projection3m: USD/ZMW 16.50–18.00 range. Expect continued appreciation bias but with increased volatility ahead of August 2026 elections.
trendAnalysis.projection6m: USD/ZMW 16.00–19.00 range. Post-election clarity determines direction — policy continuity supports kwacha, fiscal slippage reverses gains.
